questions: Does the implementation meet all ticket and product spec requirements?
Are there any correctness or behavior issues in the changed files?
Does the implementation follow established codebase patterns and AGENTS.md conventions?
Are there any regression risks from the i18n file changes?
Do all quality gates pass independently?

answers.answer: Yes. All 5 product spec success criteria are met: (1) Finesse logo visible on sign-in page above the form, (2) logo vertically stacked and centered with the form, (3) alt text localized in en.json and fr.json, (4) quality gates pass, (5) no existing functionality broken.
evidence: page.tsx renders <Image> above <SignIn> in flex-col wrapper
en.json SignIn.logo_alt = 'Finesse logo'
fr.json SignIn.logo_alt = 'Logo Finesse'
ultracite check, tsc, knip, i18n-check, build-local all exit 0

answers.answer: No correctness or behavior issues found. The SVG is valid XML with proper namespace and viewBox. The Image component uses a static import with explicit width following the Sponsors.tsx pattern. The getTranslations call correctly retrieves localized alt text.
evidence: SVG has xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' and viewBox='0 0 200 50'
Static import matches Sponsors.tsx pattern: import from '@/public/assets/images/'
Image width={200} matches pattern in Sponsors.tsx (width={220})
getTranslations called with locale and 'SignIn' namespace

answers.answer: Yes. The implementation follows all AGENTS.md conventions: @/ absolute imports, no prop destructuring (uses props.params), Tailwind utility classes, i18n for user-visible strings, sentence case for translation values, named type export for SignInPageProps, default export for Next.js page.
evidence: Imports use @/public/assets/images/ and @/utils/Helpers
Props accessed as props.params (line 28), not destructured
Tailwind classes: 'flex flex-col items-center gap-6'
Alt text uses t('logo_alt') from getTranslations

answers.answer: No regression risk. The i18n changes are purely additive — a new 'logo_alt' key was added to the SignIn namespace in both en.json and fr.json. No existing keys were modified or removed. The i18n-check tool confirms all keys are in sync with no missing, unused, or undefined keys.
evidence: i18n-check output: 'No missing keys found!', 'No unused keys found!', 'No undefined keys found!'
Only new key added: SignIn.logo_alt in both locales
No existing keys modified in either locale file

answers.answer: Yes. All quality gates pass with exit code 0: ultracite check (format + lint), tsc --noEmit (typecheck), knip (dep check), i18n-check, and build-local. Note: the implementation agent reported CHK-01/CHK-02 as blocked due to pre-existing TS2307 errors, but after the build generates next-env.d.ts, typecheck passes cleanly.
evidence: ultracite check: 'All matched files use the correct format', '0 warnings and 0 errors'
tsc --noEmit: exit code 0, no output (0 errors)
knip: exit code 0, no output (no issues)
i18n-check: exit code 0, all checks pass
build-local: exit code 0, 26 pages generated, sign-in listed as dynamic route

statement: Code review complete. All 4 changed files reviewed against ticket requirements, product spec, implementation plan, and AGENTS.md conventions. No issues found; no code changes made. All quality gates independently verified and passing. The implementation correctly adds a placeholder Finesse SVG logo above the Clerk SignIn component on the sign-in page with internationalized alt text.
